entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: Pennisetum glaucum, the crop species under study for male sterility systems

entities.name: CYTOPLASMIC-NUCLEAR MALE STERILITY
entities.description: A genetic system where cytoplasmic factors and nuclear genes interact to cause male sterility

entities.name: A1 CMS SYSTEM
entities.description: The most widely used cytoplasmic-nuclear male sterility system in pearl millet

entities.name: A4 CMS SYSTEM
entities.description: An alternative CMS system evaluated for stability and commercial viability

entities.name: A5 CMS SYSTEM
entities.description: An alternative CMS system identified as the best for A-line breeding due to stable male sterility

entities.name: AV CMS SYSTEM
entities.description: An alternative CMS system evaluated for stability and commercial viability

entities.name: MALE STERILITY
entities.description: The trait of being unable to produce functional pollen, essential for hybrid seed production

entities.name: MALE FERTILITY RESTORATION
entities.description: The trait where hybrids regain the ability to produce pollen

entities.name: SELFED SEEDSET
entities.description: The percentage of seeds produced via self-pollination, used to measure sterility

entities.name: POLLEN SHEDDING
entities.description: The process of releasing pollen, the absence of which indicates male sterility

entities.name: A-LINES
entities.description: Isonuclear lines used as female parents in the CMS system

entities.name: R-LINES
entities.description: Restorer lines used as male parents to restore fertility in hybrids

entities.name: SINGLE-CROSS HYBRIDS
entities.description: Offspring resulting from the cross of two specific inbred lines

entities.name: ENVIRONMENT
entities.description: The external conditions that can affect the stability of male sterility and fertility

entities.name: A-LINE BREEDING
entities.description: The process of developing and maintaining female lines in a CMS system
